info.name: API Testing Practice Collection
info.description:
A comprehensive Postman collection for practicing API testing skills. Covers GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE methods, authentication, query/path parameters, request headers, pre-request scripts, test assertions, chaining requests, error handling, and more.

Public APIs used:
- JSONPlaceholder (https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com)
- ReqRes (https://reqres.in)
- httpbin (https://httpbin.org)

Import this collection into Postman along with the environment file to get started.

item.name: 01 - GET Requests
item.description: Practice basic and advanced GET requests including query parameters, path parameters, and response validation.

request.description: Retrieve all posts. Practice validating the response status, body structure, and array length.

exec: pm.test('Each post has required fields', function () {

request.description: Retrieve a single post by its ID. Practice path parameter usage and field-level validation.

exec: pm.test('Post ID matches requested ID', function () {
pm.test('Post has a non-empty title', function () {
pm.test('Post has a non-empty body', function () {

request.description: Retrieve posts filtered by userId query parameter. Practice query parameter filtering and result validation.

exec: pm.test('All returned posts belong to userId 1', function () {

request.description: Retrieve comments for a specific post using nested resource URL. Practice nested/related resource requests.

exec: pm.test('Response is an array of comments', function () {
pm.test('Each comment has required fields', function () {
pm.test('All comments belong to post 1', function () {

request.description: Retrieve all users. Practice validating nested objects (address, company, geo).

exec: pm.test('Each user has nested address object', function () {
pm.test('Each user has a company object', function () {

request.description: Retrieve a paginated list of users from ReqRes. Practice validating pagination metadata and data arrays.

exec: pm.test('Data array length matches per_page', function () {
pm.test('Each user has avatar URL', function () {

item.name: 02 - POST Requests
item.description: Practice creating resources with POST requests, sending JSON bodies, and validating creation responses.

request.description: Create a new post with a JSON body. Practice sending POST requests and validating the created resource.

exec: pm.test('Response contains the submitted data', function () {
pm.expect(jsonData.body).to.eql('This is a test post created for API testing practice.');

request.description: Create a new user on ReqRes. Practice validating created timestamp and storing the created user ID for later use.

exec: pm.test('Response contains name and job', function () {
pm.test('Response contains id and createdAt', function () {
pm.test('createdAt is a valid ISO date', function () {
// Store the created user ID for use in subsequent requests

request.description: Successful user registration. Practice validating authentication tokens in responses.

exec: pm.test('Response contains id and token', function () {
pm.test('Token is a non-empty string', function () {

request.description: Successful login request. Practice validating authentication flow.
Send form-encoded data. Practice working with different content types and validating echoed data.

exec: pm.test('Form data is echoed back correctly', function () {

item.name: 03 - PUT & PATCH Requests
item.description: Practice updating resources using PUT (full replacement) and PATCH (partial update) requests.

request.description: Fully update a post using PUT. Practice understanding the difference between PUT and PATCH.

exec: pm.expect(jsonData.body).to.eql('This post has been fully updated via PUT request.');

request.description: Partially update a post using PATCH. Only the title field is sent. Practice partial updates.
Update a user on ReqRes with PUT. Practice validating the updatedAt timestamp.

item.name: 04 - DELETE Requests
item.description: Practice deleting resources and validating deletion responses.

request.description: Delete a post by ID. Practice validating empty response bodies and 200 status codes.

exec: pm.test('Response body is empty object', function () {

request.description: Delete a user on ReqRes. Practice validating 204 No Content responses.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 204 No Content', function () {

item.name: 05 - Negative Testing & Error Handling
item.description: Practice testing error scenarios: invalid data, missing fields, non-existent resources, and unauthorized access.

request.description: Request a user that doesn't exist. Practice validating 404 responses.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 404 Not Found', function () {
pm.test('Response body is empty object', function () {

request.description: Attempt registration without a password. Practice validating error messages and 400 status codes.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 400 Bad Request', function () {
pm.test('Error message indicates missing password', function () {

request.description: Attempt login without a password. Practice negative test case validation.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 400 Bad Request', function () {

request.description: Send a PATCH request to a GET-only endpoint. Practice testing method-not-allowed scenarios.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 405 Method Not Allowed', function () {

request.description: Request with a delayed response. Practice testing response time thresholds and timeout handling.

exec: pm.test('Response time is greater than 2000ms (due to delay)', function () {
pm.test('Response still contains valid data despite delay', function () {

item.name: 06 - Headers & Authentication
item.description: Practice working with custom headers, authentication schemes, and header validation.

request.description: Send custom headers and inspect them in the response. Practice working with headers and dynamic variables.

exec: pm.test('Accept header is set correctly', function () {
pm.test('X-Request-ID header is a valid GUID', function () {

request.description: Test Basic Authentication. Practice using Postman's auth helpers and validating authentication responses.
Test Bearer Token Authentication using a stored token variable. Practice token-based auth flows.

exec: pm.test('Token is present in response', function () {

item.name: 07 - Pre-request Scripts & Dynamic Data
item.description: Practice using pre-request scripts to generate dynamic data, set variables, and prepare requests.

request.description: Create a post with dynamically generated data from pre-request script. Practice pre-request scripting.

exec: const randomBody = 'This post was generated at ' + new Date().toISOString() + ' using a pre-request script.';
pm.test('Dynamic title is included in response', function () {

request.description: Send a request with dynamically generated timestamps and random numbers. Practice using Postman dynamic variables.

exec: pm.test('JSON body is echoed correctly', function () {

item.name: 08 - Response Validation & Schema Testing
item.description: Practice advanced response validation including JSON schema validation, data type checks, and response structure verification.

request.description: Validate the response against a JSON schema. Practice schema-based testing for API contracts.
Validate an array response against a JSON schema. Practice array schema validation.

exec: pm.test('Each post matches the schema', function () {
pm.expect(valid, 'Post at index ' + index + ' failed schema validation').to.be.true;

request.description: Validate specific response headers. Practice header assertion techniques.

item.name: 09 - Request Chaining & Workflows
item.description: Practice chaining requests together where data from one request is used in the next. This simulates real-world testing workflows.

request.description: Step 1: Create a user and store the returned ID for the next request.

exec: pm.test('User is created with correct data', function () {
// Chain: store ID for next request

request.description: Step 2: Update the user created in Step 1 using the stored user ID.
Step 3: Delete the user created in Step 1. Complete the CRUD workflow.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 204 No Content', function () {
console.log('CRUD workflow complete - user created, updated, and deleted.');

item.name: 10 - Advanced Techniques
item.description: Advanced testing patterns including data-driven testing, response time checks, cookie handling, redirect testing, and environment-based testing.

request.description: Test specific HTTP status codes. Change the path parameter to test different codes (200, 201, 301, 400, 404, 500).

exec: pm.test('Status code is in 2xx range', function () {

request.description: Set and validate cookies. Practice cookie-based testing (enable 'Follow redirects' in Postman settings).
Get client IP information. Practice validating IP format in responses.

exec: pm.test('Origin is a valid IP address', function () {

request.description: Receive and validate an XML response. Practice testing non-JSON response formats.

exec: pm.test('Response body contains XML content', function () {

request.description: Receive a gzip-compressed response. Practice handling compressed responses.

exec: pm.test('Response contains origin and headers', function () {

request.description: Demonstrate comprehensive assertion patterns covering status, headers, body structure, data types, and business logic.

exec: pm.test('Response has expected top-level keys', function () {
pm.test('All user emails contain @ symbol', function () {
pm.test('No user has null or undefined name', function () {
